overview: Dr. K.V. Subba Reddy Institute of Pharmacy (KVSP), established in 2007, is a premier pharmacy institution located in Kurnool, Andhra Pradesh. Part of the Dr. K.V. Subba Reddy Group of Institutions, the college is dedicated to providing high-quality pharmaceutical education and research opportunities. It is known for its strong emphasis on clinical pharmacy and industry-readiness in the Rayalaseema region.
accreditation: Approved by the Pharmacy Council of India (PCI) and AICTE. Affiliated with Jawaharlal Nehru Technological University, Anantapur (JNTUA).

coursesAndFees.eligibility: 10+2 with 45% aggregate in PCB/PCM + AP EAPCET rank
10+2 with Physics & Chemistry as compulsory subjects + AP EAPCET rank
B.Pharmacy with 50% aggregate + GPAT / AP PGECET score
10+2 pass with Physics, Chemistry, and Biology/Mathematics

admissions.admissionProcess: Admissions are strictly merit-based. 70% of seats (Category A) are filled via state-level counseling (APSCHE) based on entrance ranks. 30% (Category B) are Management Quota seats filled based on institutional merit. Counseling Code: KVSP.
admissions.importantDates: AP EAPCET 2026 Pharmacy Exam: May 19–20, 2026; Admit Cards: Available from April 30, 2026; Results: Late May 2026.

facilities: On-campus Separate Hostels for Boys and Girls
Transportation (Fleet covering Kurnool city and surrounding areas)
campus.specialNote: The institute is strategically located opposite the Dupadu Railway Station on NH-44. It features a dedicated Training & Placement Cell that provides specialized training in Medical Coding, Pharmacovigilance (PV), and Clinical Research to ensure graduates are globally competitive.

contact.address: Opp: Dupadu Railway Station, NH-44, Lakshmipuram Post, Kurnool, Andhra Pradesh - 518218
